success_surface: Extracted duplicate records using hybrid pandas/openpyxl approach with proper type coercion, safe style copying, and formatted output
effective_behaviors: Used pandas for data transformation (filtering, grouping, sorting) and openpyxl for workbook structure/styling preservation
Handled openpyxl object properties safely without using copy.deepcopy() on proxy-style objects
Converted non-standard date strings to datetime objects before sorting to ensure chronological order
Applied formatting selectively (borders, column widths, frozen panes) to improve readability while avoiding style corruption
Checked for existing target sheets before creation or deletion to prevent conflicts
Used try/except blocks with traceback logging for graceful error recovery during development iterations
Printed intermediate diagnostic outputs (row counts, filtered values, sorted results) to verify correctness at each step
evidence: Initial RecursionError traced to copy.deepcopy() on openpyxl.font.Font proxy object; removed deepcopy and replaced with direct value assignment
Successfully processed duplicate detection via pandas value_counts(), filtering, and multi-column sorting
Applied openpyxl cell formatting (number_format, border styles, column_widths, freeze_panes) only where necessary for presentation
Created new output sheet after deleting any pre-existing one to ensure clean state
Final output verified with row count confirmation (4 duplicates) and file path validation

memory_items.title: Hybrid Library Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Use pandas for data manipulation logic and openpyxl for structural/stylistic workbook changes
memory_items.generalization: Load data into pandas for filtering, aggregation, and sorting operations. Use openpyxl only when preserving or modifying workbook-level elements (sheets, formulas, charts, cell formatting) that pandas cannot handle.

memory_items.title: Safe OpenPyXL Object Manipulation
memory_items.lesson: Avoid copy.deepcopy() on openpyxl style/proxy objects due to internal recursion issues
memory_items.generalization: When cloning cell styles from source worksheets, create new style instances directly instead of deep-closing existing ones. For fonts, fills, borders, explicitly construct new objects with desired attributes.

memory_items.title: Type-Aware Sorting
memory_items.lesson: Ensure date columns are converted to datetime before sorting to avoid string-based lexicographic errors
memory_items.generalization: Before sorting any date/time column, use pd.to_datetime() with errors='coerce' to standardize format, sort on the converted column, then drop auxiliary temporary columns from final output.

memory_items.title: Diagnostic Output Logging
memory_items.lesson: Print intermediate results (counts, sample values, ranges) during multi-step transformations
memory_items.generalization: Include print statements showing dataset shape, filtered subsets, unique value lists, and result dimensions to enable quick validation of each processing stage.

memory_items.title: Sheet State Management
memory_items.lesson: Always verify sheet existence before creating or deleting to prevent runtime errors
memory_items.generalization: Check wb.sheetnames before accessing or modifying worksheet names. Delete target sheets before recreating if a fresh start is required.

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons

### Hybrid Library Optimization
- **Pandas + OpenPyXL Split**: Use pandas for all data operations (filtering, aggregations, sorting); reserve openpyxl exclusively for workbook-level modifications (sheet creation, cell formatting, formula injection).
- **Performance Note**: Large datasets (>10K rows) benefit significantly from pandas filtering before openpyxl writes.

### Style & Property Safety
- **No Deep Copy on Styles**: Never use `copy.deepcopy()` on openpyxl style objects (font, fill, border) — they often contain recursive proxy structures. Instead, read attribute values and reconstruct new style objects.
- **Formula Preservation**: When moving cells with formulas, preserve the formula string itself (`cell.value = '=SUM(...)'`) rather than converting to calculated value.

### Robust Execution Patterns
- **Pre-flight Checks**: Validate sheet names, column headers, and data types before attempting transformations.
- **Iterative Verification**: Print key metrics after each major step (rows processed, unique values found, duplicates identified) to catch errors early.
- **Exception Guarding**: Wrap core execution logic in try/except blocks with detailed traceback printing for debugging cycles.
- **Output Validation**: Confirm final row/column counts match expectations before marking task complete.
